heading: Coordination of a case under this title and a foreign proceeding
label: 1529 Coordination of a case under this title and a foreign proceeding
notes: Amendments2010—Par. (1). , which directed amendment of par. (1) by inserting “is” after “States”, was executed by making the insertion only in introductory provisions to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 111–327

Effective DateSection effective 180 days after , and not applicable with respect to cases commenced under this title before such effective date, except as otherwise provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2005 Amendment note under .Apr. 20, 2005section 1501 of Pub. L. 109–8section 101 of this title
amendments: Amendments2010—Par. (1). , which directed amendment of par. (1) by inserting “is” after “States”, was executed by making the insertion only in introductory provisions to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 111–327

Effective DateSection effective 180 days after , and not applicable with respect to cases commenced under this title before such effective date, except as otherwise provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2005 Amendment note under .Apr. 20, 2005section 1501 of Pub. L. 109–8section 101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any relief granted under section 1519 or 1521 must be consistent with the relief granted in the case in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: section 1520 does not apply even if such foreign proceeding is recognized as a foreign main proceeding.

children.label: A
children.text: any relief in effect under section 1519 or 1521 shall be reviewed by the court and shall be modified or terminated if inconsistent with the case in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: if such foreign proceeding is a foreign main proceeding, the stay and suspension referred to in section 1520(a) shall be modified or terminated if inconsistent with the relief granted in the case in the United States.

In granting, extending, or modifying relief granted to a representative of a foreign nonmain proceeding, the court must be satisfied that the relief relates to assets that, under the laws of the United States, should be administered in the foreign nonmain proceeding or concerns information required in that proceeding.
In achieving cooperation and coordination under sections 1528 and 1529, the court may grant any of the relief authorized under section 305.
